mechanism_narrative: Sustained growth in real retail sales (RSAFS) signals robust consumer spending, which drives corporate earnings and lifts equity prices. The trend is always active, with rising RSAFS predicting positive S&P 500 forward returns.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 3-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns are not significantly positive during RSAFS expansion phases (p>0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A rise in the unemployment rate above a 5% threshold signals labor market weakness that reduces aggregate demand and corporate profits, leading to negative S&P 500 forward returns.
failure_condition: S&P 500 generates positive 20-day returns in more than 50% of instances after UNRATE crosses above 5%, or negative returns are not statistically significant (t-test p>0.10).

mechanism_narrative: When the federal funds rate is rising at the same time that the unemployment rate is falling, the economy is overheating, prompting further tightening and eventually slowing growth, which depresses equity prices. The joint behavior is necessary for the signal.
candidate_trigger: FEDFUNDS 3-month moving average increases while UNRATE 3-month moving average decreases over the same period
failure_condition: In quarters where both conditions hold simultaneously, 20-day forward returns are positive more than 40% of the time, or the joint signal has no significant negative returns (one-sided test p>0.05).

mechanism_narrative: During a monetary tightening cycle, increases in the federal funds rate compress valuations and raise risk premiums, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns. The relationship reverses in easing cycles, but under the current tightening regime the effect is negative.
failure_condition: During identified tightening cycles (e.g., 2004-2006), S&P 500 20-day returns are positive more than half the time after each rate increase, failing to support the negative directional hypothesis.

mechanism_narrative: Growing industrial production signals strong manufacturing activity and economic expansion, which supports corporate earnings and drives short-term gains in equities. The trend is always active.
failure_condition: 5-day forward returns following positive INDPRO momentum (3-month increase) are not significantly positive (mean return < 0 with p>0.10), or Hit Rate of positive returns below 55%.

mechanism_narrative: A sharp decline in housing starts below a key threshold signals deterioration in residential investment, a leading indicator for economic weakness, which translates into negative S&P 500 returns over the next quarter.
candidate_trigger: HOUST 3-month average falls below 1700 thousand units
failure_condition: After HOUST drops below 1700, the average 60-day S&P 500 return is positive, or returns are negative but not statistically different from zero (t-test p>0.10) over a 2-year rolling evaluation.

mechanism_narrative: When consumer spending (retail sales) is declining while the central bank is raising interest rates, the dual headwinds of weakening demand and tightening financial conditions create a negative impulse for equities. The joint deterioration is required for the signal.
candidate_trigger: RSAFS 3-month MA declines simultaneously with FEDFUNDS 3-month MA rising over the same period
failure_condition: At least 40% of joint trigger episodes produce positive 20-day returns, or the negative return distribution is not statistically significantly below zero (one-sided t-test p>0.05).

mechanism_narrative: In a low unemployment regime (UNRATE below 5%), further increases in payroll employment signal labor market tightness, rising wage pressures, and potential margin compression, which weighs on equity valuations. The regime condition determines the sign of the relationship.
regime_hint: low unemployment regime (UNRATE below 5% for at least 3 months)
failure_condition: During periods of UNRATE below 5%, months with above-trend PAYEMS growth (monthly change > 150k) have average 20-day returns that are positive, or the negative returns are not significant (p>0.10) over a 3-year rolling window.
